flashcards.front: What is the fundamental difference between combinational and sequential circuits?
flashcards.back: Combinational circuits produce output based only on current inputs (no memory), while sequential circuits produce output based on current inputs AND past inputs (have memory elements).
A flip-flop is a bistable sequential circuit that stores one bit of data. It has two stable states (0 or 1) and can be triggered by clock pulses.
A multiplexer is a combinational circuit that selects one of multiple input lines and directs it to a single output line based on select line inputs.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Mealy and Moore state machines?
flashcards.back: Mealy machine: output depends on current state AND current input. Moore machine: output depends only on current state. Moore has one more clock cycle delay than Mealy.
A latch is a level-sensitive memory element that continuously monitors its inputs and changes output whenever inputs change (not clock-triggered).

flashcards.front: Define propagation delay in digital circuits.
flashcards.back: Propagation delay is the time taken for a change in input to produce a change in output. It limits the maximum operating frequency of digital circuits.
A half adder is a combinational circuit that adds two 1-bit numbers. It has two inputs (A, B) and produces SUM and CARRY outputs using XOR and AND gates.
A full adder adds three 1-bit inputs (A, B, and Cin) to produce SUM and CARRY outputs. It can be constructed using two half adders and an OR gate.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of a clock signal in sequential circuits?
flashcards.back: The clock signal synchronizes the operation of sequential circuits, ensuring all flip-flops sample and update their states at precise moments to maintain proper timing.
A decoder is a combinational circuit that converts n input lines into 2^n output lines. Only one output is HIGH (or LOW) at any time based on the binary input code.
An encoder performs the reverse operation of a decoder—it converts 2^n input lines into n output lines, producing binary code corresponding to the active input.
A synchronous counter is a sequential circuit where all flip-flops are clocked simultaneously by the same clock signal, ensuring all bits change at the same time.
An asynchronous counter (ripple counter) uses the output of one flip-flop as the clock for the next, causing cumulative propagation delay.

flashcards.front: Define setup time and hold time.
flashcards.back: Setup time: minimum time input must be stable before clock edge. Hold time: minimum time input must remain stable after clock edge. Violating these causes metastability.
A shift register is a sequential circuit that stores and shifts data bits serially or in parallel. It can perform serial-to-parallel and parallel-to-serial conversion.
